  • Tutorials
  • Tutorial: Ventilation & Humidity Control
  • Tutorial: Ventilation & Humidity Control

Tutorial: Ventilation & Humidity Control

GreenhousesJL models natural and forced ventilation with dynamic humidity tracking, matching the Greenhouses Modelica Library (Boulard & Baille 1995).

Ventilation model

The air exchange rate combines wind-driven and buoyancy-driven flow (NaturalVentilationRate_2.mo):

\[f_{vent} = U_{roof} \times \eta_{roof} \times \frac{C_d}{2} \times \sqrt{\frac{g \cdot h_{vent}}{2} \cdot \frac{|\Delta T|}{T_{mean}} + C_w \cdot u^2}\]

Plus a permanent leakage term: $f_{leak} = \max(0.25, u) \times c_{leakage}$

Presets

PresetType$\eta_{roof}$$h_{vent}$RH control
VenloVent()Standard Venlo10%0.68 mNo
VenloVentRH()Venlo + humidity10%0.68 mRH > 85%
TunnelVent()Multi-span tunnel20%1.20 mNo
TunnelVentRH()Tunnel + humidity20%1.20 mRH > 85%
NoVent()Sealed00No

Temperature + humidity control (Korner & Challa 2003)

Vent opening is the maximum of temperature and humidity demands:

U_roof = max(U_temperature, U_humidity)

This matches the Modelica implementation (Uvents_RH_T_Mdot.mo): ventilation opens on whichever demand is higher.

RH control prevents Botrytis and condensation but adds 5-15% heating energy in winter (Korner 2003).

Usage

using GreenhousesJL

tmy = load_tmy("path/to/weather.txt")

# Temperature-only ventilation
p = GreenhouseParams(tmy; A=200.0,
    Kp=20000.0, Ti=300.0, T_sp=291.15, Q_max=40000.0,
    ventilation=VenloVent())

# Temperature + humidity control
p = GreenhouseParams(tmy; A=200.0,
    Kp=20000.0, Ti=300.0, T_sp=291.15, Q_max=40000.0,
    ventilation=VenloVentRH(),
    crop=CropModel(LAI=2.5))  # crop needed for transpiration

sol = solve(ODEProblem(greenhouse_rhs!, initial_state(p), (0.0, 7*86400.0), p),
    Tsit5(); reltol=1e-6, saveat=3600.0, maxiters=5_000_000)

EnergyPlus benchmark (Lyon July, 14,000 m2)

ScenarioEP T_moyJL T_moy$\Delta T$JL RHJL CO2
Sealed19.924.2+4.370%420 ppm
Leakage 0.5 ACH20.323.7+3.463%420 ppm
Venlo T>2520.522.5+2.069%420 ppm
Venlo T>2221.222.3+1.169%420 ppm
Tunnel T>2221.222.4+1.268%420 ppm
T+RH + tomaton/a20.5n/a86%407 ppm
T+RH + CO2 800n/a20.5n/a86%627 ppm

With active ventilation, the temperature agreement is 1-2 degrees C, consistent with the heating benchmark.

CO2 dynamics

CO2 is tracked as an ODE state variable. Canopy photosynthesis depletes CO2, ventilation exchanges with outdoor air (420 ppm), and optional enrichment injects CO2.

# CO2 enrichment to 800 ppm
p = GreenhouseParams(tmy; A=200.0,
    ventilation=VenloVentRH(),
    co2=CO2Enrichment(target=800.0, rate=7.0),
    crop=CropModel(LAI=2.5))

Sealed greenhouse with tomato: CO2 drops to 50 ppm (compensation point) within hours.

Humidity balance

Vapour pressure (VP_air) is an ODE state driven by:

  • Transpiration (source, Stanghellini model)
  • Ventilation (sink/source, depending on outdoor VP)
  • Condensation on cover (sink, when VPair > satvp(T_cover))

References

  • Boulard, T. & Baille, A. (1995). J. Agric. Eng. Research, 61, 37-48
  • Korner, O. & Challa, H. (2003). Comp. Elec. Agriculture, 39, 173-192
  • Campen, J.B., Bot, G.P.A. & de Zwart, H.F. (2003). Biosyst. Eng., 86(4), 487-493
  • Stanghellini, C. (1987). PhD thesis, Wageningen University
